Tests the `String.fromCharCode()` function to ensure it returns an empty string when no arguments are given.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `String.fromCharCode` method when called without arguments. The `assert` function verifies that the result of `String.fromCharCode()` is an empty string `""`. This test ensures that the method correctly returns an empty string when no character codes are provided, which is consistent with the expected behavior of the JavaScript `String.fromCharCode` function. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `String.fromCharCode` method.
